channel.title: INtopBackup
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INtopBackup, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Urban sketchers find the sublime in the city block
item.description: Sketchers say making art together in urban environments allows them to create a record of a moment and to notice a little bit more about the city they...

item.title: How a recent shift in DNA sleuthing might help investigators in the Nancy Guthrie case
item.description: DNA science has helped solve criminal cases for decades. But increasingly, investigative genetic genealogy — which was first used for cold cases — is ...

item.title: How do Olympians like Eileen Gu earn $23 million? Here are all the ways
item.description: Most Olympians never win big money — or big fame. So how are athletes such as Eileen Gu or Chloe Kim able to earn millions of dollars? Here are some o...

item.title: Epstein once attended an elite arts camp. Years later, he used it to find his victims
item.description: Jeffrey Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell lavished money on the Interlochen Center for the Arts to gain access, documents show — even funding an on-campus...

item.title: An unsung hero stepped in to help a newly widowed mom in a moment of need
item.description: Barbara Alvarez lost her husband in 2017, just before their daughter went off to college. Her unsung hero helped her find the strength to be a single ...

item.title: Former South Korean President Yoon gets life sentence for imposing martial law
item.description: Former President Yoon Suk Yeol was sentenced to life in prison for briefly imposing martial law in a dramatic culmination to the country's biggest pol...

item.title: Do not give away Diego Garcia, Trump tells UK in fresh attack on Chagos deal
item.description: The president's comments come just a day after the US gave its official backing to the UK's Chagos deal.

item.title: Skiers with roots in Lake Tahoe’s alpine community among 8 killed in California avalanche
item.description: Authorities say the bodies of eight backcountry skiers have been found and one remains missing after an avalanche near Lake Tahoe in California.

item.title: Inside the surge of threats against public officials fueling a rise in prosecutions
item.description: A CBS News analysis found 126 cases that were brought by federal prosecutors last year arising out of threats to public officials.

item.title: Billionaire Les Wexner tells US lawmakers he was 'naive' and 'conned' by Epstein
item.description: Testifying before Congress, the former CEO of Victoria's Secret lingerie brand accused Epstein of stealing "vast sums" of money from his family.

item.title: See the Olympics schedule for the 2026 Milano Cortina Games
item.description: This is what you need to know about when the 2026 Milano Cortina Winter Olympics start and end, along with key games and events on the schedule.

item.title: Federal tax dollars subsidize health care plans in ways you may not realize
item.description: Critics have questioned why the federal government should underwrite coverage costs for people with ACA health plans — but almost all health insurance...

item.title: Snow drought helped set the stage for deadly California avalanche, leading to unstable conditions
South Korea's ex-president jailed for life over martial law attempt
item.description: Yoon Suk Yeol's December 2024 martial law bid fundamentally damaged South Korea's democracy, a judge told the court.

item.title: Samba school that praised Brazil's Lula at Rio Carnival relegated
item.description: See the floats from the famous festival as the city's top samba schools compete for first prize.

item.title: Giant robot tribute to Brazil's President Lula comes last in carnival competition
item.description: Rio de Janeiro's iconic Carnival parades began with a tribute to Brazil's President Lula, illustrating his rise from poverty to power.

item.title: Former Prince Andrew arrested over Epstein files revelations
item.description: Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor, King Charles III's younger brother, has been arrested over suspected misconduct in public office after revelations in the ...

item.title: The backcountry ski trip hit by avalanche was led by an outfit offering mountain adventures globally
